title: [Bug]: Oracle NoSQL KVLite helper should wait for SDK readiness
summary: The new Oracle NoSQL Database KVLite smoke helper can treat an open TCP proxy port as readiness before the Oracle NoSQL Python SDK can complete a table request.
observed: During local issue #310 container smoke testing, the helper reached the SDK table creation step after the proxy accepted TCP connections, but the SDK received a disconnected HTTP response from the local KVLite proxy.
expected: The helper should wait for Oracle NoSQL SDK-level readiness, retry transient proxy startup failures within the bounded timeout, and only run smoke or sink e2e writes after a simple table operation can complete.
reproduction: Run python scripts/run-oracle-nosql-container-smoke.py against a freshly started Oracle NoSQL Database KVLite container where the proxy socket opens before SDK requests are fully ready.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test that simulates transient SDK readiness failures and asserts the helper retries before reporting readiness.
impact: The defect affects local test orchestration only. It can make issue #310 and issue #149 container-backed verification flaky or fail early, but it does not change production sink delivery semantics or ACK behavior.
delivery_semantics: The defect occurs before any JetStream message is consumed. It does not alter sink write ordering, idempotency, DLQ handling, or commit-then-ACK behavior.
security: The fix must keep retry evidence sanitized and must not print local container names, payloads, credentials, or private endpoint details.
acceptance: The helper performs SDK-level readiness checks after the TCP port opens.
Transient SDK readiness failures are retried within the configured bounded timeout.
Persistent readiness failures still fail closed with a concise sanitized error.
The Oracle NoSQL container smoke test and sink e2e helper use the SDK-level readiness check.
Focused unit tests cover transient and persistent readiness behavior without requiring Docker.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_oracle_nosql_test_container.py -q, then rerun python scripts/run-oracle-nosql-container-smoke.py and python scripts/run-oracle-nosql-sink-e2e.py when Docker and the optional Oracle NoSQL Python SDK are available.
documentation: No public command syntax changes are required. Test evidence and the test backend documentation should mention SDK-level readiness.
closeout: Mark completed after the readiness retry fix is merged back into the issue #310 feature branch with sanitized unit and container evidence.
